atoms.semantic_claim: The downstream Reth EVM crate added a test-only async-context module that imports revm_context async state types and primitive fixtures directly, so the Reth EVM test build exercised async-context API compatibility from the crate boundary.

destinations.evidence: Lines 600-674 contain #[cfg(test)] unit tests for AsyncCommitmentutionContext in the revm-context crate.
Lines 702-769 contain #[cfg(all(test, feature = "async-context"))] tests for AsyncContextTr integration.

atoms.equivalence_argument: The old Reth EVM crate-boundary test module is not present in the v2 port, but the same async-context type is now owned and tested in ritual-revm-internal. This covers type-local behavior, not Reth EVM crate registration, so the mapping is dependency-local rather than exact.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Disable or remove revm-context async-context tests and confirm the Reth v2 async-context feature graph still has a crate-boundary test that imports the context API.
atoms.notes: Reviewed: crate-boundary test moved to dependency-local tests. Missing exact Reth EVM test registration is test/support only.

atoms.semantic_claim: A default AsyncCommitmentutionContext starts in Building mode with SPC call index 0 and no execution results.

destinations.evidence: Lines 253-269 implement Default with execution_mode default, spc_call_index 0, and empty execution_results; lines 604-615 test ExecutionMode::default(), AsyncCommitmentutionContext::new(), Building mode, index 0, and empty execution_results.

atoms.equivalence_argument: The destination tests validate the same default-state semantics through the type-owned new()/getter APIs instead of the old Reth EVM direct-field assertions. Because the coverage moved to the revm context dependency and is not textually identical, it is non-exact.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Change the default execution mode, initial SPC call index, or execution_results initialization and confirm ritual-revm-internal context tests fail.
atoms.notes: Reviewed: default Building mode, spc_call_index 0, and empty execution_results are implemented and tested in revm-context.

atoms.semantic_claim: A default AsyncCommitmentutionContext has no pending SPC calls and no async metadata before transaction execution or fulfilled replay setup.

destinations.evidence: Lines 253-269 initialize pending_spc_calls to Vec::new() and async_metadata to None, but the test block at lines 604-674 does not assert either default.
Lines 793-805 populate pending_spc_calls and async_metadata for fulfilled original replay, making the default-empty invariant relevant to replay setup.
Lines 294-305 skip installing verification context when async_metadata is already present or pending_spc_calls is non-empty, so stale default state would affect verification context installation.

atoms.equivalence_argument: The destination code implements and consumes this default-empty invariant, but exact-code and structural test searches found no destination assertion equivalent to the source test's pending_spc_calls.is_empty() and async_metadata.is_none() checks.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Seed a default context with stale pending_spc_calls or async_metadata and verify a destination unit test fails before fulfilled replay or verification context installation proceeds.
atoms.notes: Reviewed: runtime defaults are implemented in AsyncCommitmentutionContext::default, but the exact pending_spc_calls.is_empty() and async_metadata.is_none() assertions are still missing. Test-only gap, not a runtime FSM gap.

atoms.semantic_claim: The async context execution mode can transition between Building and Verification and back, preserving the enum identity used to choose builder versus verifier async-precompile behavior.

destinations.evidence: Lines 278-294 expose with_mode/building/verification constructors; lines 617-626 test Building and Verification mode predicates.
Lines 81-89 assert a verification context is not building, is verification, and has ExecutionMode::Verification.
Lines 89-96 assert the same verification-mode identity for JQ timeout handling.

atoms.equivalence_argument: Destination tests cover both execution-mode identities and mode predicates through constructors and precompile verification tests, but they do not exactly repeat direct field mutation from Building to Verification and back in one context.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Make verification() return Building or make is_verification() false and confirm revm-context and nodebuilder precompile verification tests fail.
atoms.notes: Reviewed: constructor/predicate coverage for Building and Verification mode is sufficient for the v2 API shape; direct field assignment is not required.

atoms.semantic_claim: Async precompile execution results can be stored by precompile address and retrieved byte-for-byte from the async context.

destinations.evidence: Lines 326-334 implement add_execution_result/get_execution_result; lines 643-654 test storing output bytes by address and returning None for a missing address.
Lines 761-769 test mutable async_context access plus execution-result storage through AsyncContextTr.

atoms.equivalence_argument: The destination dependency tests cover the same result-map semantics through public helper methods and add an absent-address negative check. The old source used direct HashMap insertion, so this is moved dependency coverage rather than exact textual coverage.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Corrupt result storage by address or return a result for an absent address and confirm ritual-revm-internal context tests fail.
atoms.notes: Reviewed: execution-result storage by precompile address is implemented and tested in revm-context through helper APIs.

atoms.semantic_claim: Each async precompile dispatch can advance the SPC call index monotonically from 0 to 1 to 2, preserving replay ordering for pending SPC calls.

destinations.evidence: Lines 311-319 implement spc_call_index and increment_spc_call_index; lines 628-641 test 0 -> 1 -> 2 and reset to 0.
Lines 680-683 expose current_spc_call_index through AsyncContextTr; lines 742-759 test incrementing through the context trait from 1 to 2.
Lines 157-162 read current_spc_call_index before pending_spc_calls lookup and line 225 increments the index after dispatch.

atoms.equivalence_argument: The destination revm-context dependency tests preserve the same monotonic index behavior and additionally cover reset and trait access. It is non-exact because the test moved from Reth EVM to revm-context and uses getter APIs.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Make increment_spc_call_index a no-op or increment by two and confirm revm-context tests fail and precompile replay order would desynchronize.
atoms.notes: Reviewed: SPC call index increment/reset is implemented and tested in revm-context.

atoms.semantic_claim: The async context can retain a pending SpcCall fixture with precompile address, input, output, proof, and block/runtime metadata, and equality preserves the exact queued call.

destinations.evidence: Lines 351-371 implement add_spc_call, pending_spc_calls(), and take_pending_spc_calls; lines 567-576 populate verification_context from SpcCall values, but the test block at lines 604-674 does not assert pending call push/length/equality.
Lines 14-25 define SpcCall fields address/input/output/proof/block_number and lines 27-37 construct those five fields.
Lines 615-619 read current_spc_call_index and pending_spc_calls for precompile dispatch, showing the queue is consumed by async precompile verification.

atoms.equivalence_argument: Destination code has the pending-SPC queue and uses it during verification, but exact-code-first searches did not find an equivalent destination test that pushes a SpcCall into AsyncCommitmentutionContext and asserts queue length/equality.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Add a revm-context test that creates a destination SpcCall, adds it through add_spc_call, asserts pending_spc_calls().len() == 1, equality, and take_pending_spc_calls drains it.
atoms.notes: Reviewed: pending_spc_calls runtime storage exists, but the exact queued SpcCall equality fixture is still absent. Test-only gap; no runtime missing implementation found.

atoms.semantic_claim: The old source fixture expected SpcCall construction to carry program_counter and caller_address identity data alongside the async precompile call payload.

destinations.evidence: Lines 14-25 define only address, input, output, proof, and block_number; lines 27-37 SpcCall::new accepts only those five values.

Lines 58-64 explicitly document that program_counter and caller_address validation was removed because those fields were removed from TxAsyncCommitment, and lines 93-99 construct SpcCall without PC/caller fields.
Lines 432-433 comment that program_counter and caller_address fields were removed as part of async settlement work.

atoms.equivalence_argument: The destination repos intentionally removed PC/caller from async commitment and SpcCall construction, so there is no exact or structural test equivalent for preserving those fields. The destination evidence supports intentional absence, but the consensus/runtime identity semantics changed and require review.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Review whether destination async settlement has an alternative identity binding for caller/program-counter-sensitive precompile calls, or add a regression asserting these fields are deliberately not part of SpcCall encoding.
atoms.notes: Reviewed: old program_counter/caller_address fixture fields are intentionally absent from the current SpcCall design; no v2 runtime gap.

adversarial_reviews.finding: All positive async-context coverage is non-exact because it moved from a Reth EVM crate-boundary test file to ritual-revm-internal type-local tests.

adversarial_reviews.scope: pending SPC default and queue tests
adversarial_reviews.finding: Destination code initializes and consumes pending_spc_calls and async_metadata, but exact-code-first searches found no equivalent tests for default emptiness or pending SpcCall push/equality.

The old source SpcCall fixture included program_counter and caller_address; destination evidence says those fields were intentionally removed, which is high-risk consensus/runtime drift and needs adversarial review.

summary: Mechanical review complete. The old Reth EVM async_context_tests.rs file is not present, but the runtime async-context invariants are implemented and mostly tested in ritual-revm-internal and nodebuilder precompile tests. Remaining misses are test-only assertions for default pending_spc_calls/async_metadata emptiness and queued SpcCall equality; no production FSM gap was found.
notes: Reviewed against latest code. Treat this row as test coverage only: runtime defaults and mutation APIs exist in revm-context, and Building/Verification mode behavior is covered structurally.
